classes.name: Elementalist
classes.pitch: The elementalist uses Spirit Energy to manipulate various forms of matter - traditionally water, fire, earth, and air.
classes.examples: Avatar: The Last Airbender, Fullmetal Alchemist, Gaara (Naruto)
classes.notes: OUTDATED: table-based class data; update to universal class benefits + features.

classes.name: Henshin Hero
classes.pitch: The Henshin Hero, similar to the Magical Guardian, has two forms. They spend most of their time in their average human forms, but transform for battle. The Henshin Hero is more combat oriented than the Magical Guardian, usually having some innate armor and weapons when they transform.

classes.name: Magical Guardian
classes.pitch: The Magical Guardian uses spirit energy to defend, heal, and enhance their allies. They are primarily a support class, and their magic can provide a wide range of utility.
classes.examples: Sailor Moon, Cardcaptor Sakura, Puella Magi Madoka Magica

classes.name: Mech Pilot
classes.pitch: The Mech Pilot class revolves around channeling their spiritual energy through technology - particularly a piloted robotic vehicle known as a mech.
classes.notes: OUTDATED: table-based class data; update to universal class benefits + features.

classes.name: Metamorph
classes.pitch: The Metamorph uses spiritual energy to modify their own bodies - either whole or in part - into new forms. These new forms include elemental transformations, bestial or alien forms, growing giant, changing their body’s composition to have new material properties, etc.
classes.examples: Titan Shifters (Attack on Titan), Luffy (One Piece - Rubber material), Ben 10, Kirby

classes.name: Monster Tamer
classes.pitch: A monster tamer shares a special bond with one or more creatures that helps them both to grow stronger.
classes.examples: Ash (Pokemon), Tai (Digimon), Yugi Muto (Yu-Gi-Oh), Final Fantasy, etc.

classes.name: Ninja
classes.pitch: A character who uses spirit energy to enhance their stealth, deception, and infiltration. Ninjas are fast, tricky, mysterious, and deadly.

classes.name: Psychic
classes.pitch: The psychic primarily uses spirit energy to enhance their minds, not their bodies. They can use their minds to manipulate objects, read and project thoughts, and eventually even stop time.
classes.examples: Mob Psycho 100, Akira, Jojo’s Bizarre Adventure (Arguably)
classes.notes: OUTDATED: table-based class data; update to universal class benefits + features.

classes.name: Spirit Warrior
classes.pitch: A Spirit Warrior channels Spirit Energy to enhance their physical abilities, and can also release their Spirit Energy in various forms of attack. Focuses on close-combat against a single target. High single-target damage and good bulk. Struggles more with multiple targets and ranged abilities.
classes.examples: Goku (Dragonball), Ryu (Street Fighter), Rock Lee (Naruto), Kenshiro (Fist of the North Star)

classes.name: Weapon Master
classes.pitch: The weapon master is a master of weapons and armor. They use spirit energy to enhance their abilities both offensively and defensively. They channel energy through their weapons to perform incredible feats, such as slicing a boulder in half, deflecting bullets in midair, or swinging weapons with ease that a normal person couldn’t even lift. The weapon master focuses on battlefield control and multi-target damage. Their single-target damage isn’t bad, but doesn’t match up to classes that focus on single combat.
classes.examples: Goemon (Lupin III), Guts (Berserk), Rurouni Kenshin, Ichigo (Bleach)

henshin-hero.name: Hero Transformation
henshin-hero.description:
You have an object - such as a Crystal, Technological Device, a watch, or a weapon, that serves as your Transformation object. This object channels your energy, and allows you to transform into a powerful new form. As an action while holding this item you can transform into your Henshin Hero form. This transformation usually involves a costume change, the user being enveloped in energy, and possibly explosions. When you transform into your Hero form, increment your Power Die by 1, and you gain 1 Armor. You also gain the following ability:

Inspiring Aura
Your heroic presence and cinematic fighting style inspires your allies. While transformed, you have an aura that affects allies within 3 squares of you (including yourself). Allies in this aura gain a +1D Status Bonus to Mental Defense rolls. You leave this transformation when you drop to 0 HP, or you spend an action to transform back into your civilian form. Once you have transformed in this way, you cannot do so again until you take a short rest.

henshin-hero.name: Heroic Combat Training
henshin-hero.description: Choose one of the following combat skills: Targeting, Melee Weapons, or Martial Arts. You have a Medium skill progression in the chosen skill. If you choose Targeting or Melee Weapons, you gain 1 Rank 1 weapon that uses that skill.

henshin-hero.name: Emblem
henshin-hero.description: Choose one of the following Emblems. This emblem represents the “theme” of your transformation. Each emblem provides a benefit to the user, as well as an aura that provides a benefit to allies near you.

options.name: Wild Animal
options.description:
This emblem takes the form of a wild animal such as a lion, shark, or eagle. You gain the following action: Mark Prey
Your enhanced animalist senses allow you to detect signs of danger - allies within your inspiring aura also gain a +1D Status Bonus to Observation.

options.name: Prehistoric Beast
options.description: This emblem takes the form of an extinct prehistoric animal, such as a Dinosaur, Pteranodon, or Mammoth. Titanic Strength - You channel the massive strength and size of a prehistoric beast. Your scale is treated as +1 for the purposes of forced movement. Whenever you would forcibly push or pull another creature, increase the maximum distance by 1 square. When another creature would try to move you, you may choose to reduce that distance by 1 square. Allies in your inspiring aura gain a +1D Status Bonus to Athletics, as they channel the awe-inspiring strength of these ancient creatures.

options.name: Mythical Creature
options.description: This emblem takes the form of a legendary being such as a dragon, phoenix, or unicorn. You gain the following reaction: Mythic Rebirth

options.name: Insect
options.description:
This emblem takes the form of an inconspicuous and adaptable insect, such as a Stag Beetle, Grasshopper, or Butterfly. You gain the following ability:

Beetle Armor - You gain Armor 5 to Bleeding and Poison
Allies in your inspiring aura gain a +1D status bonus to stealth, as your aura helps camouflage them into their surroundings.

options.name: Element
options.description: This emblem takes the form of a primal element, such as Water, Fire, Earth, or Wind. Choose one of these elements. You gain Rank 1 (Beginner) training in the Elementalism skill using the chosen element, and a “Slow” skill progression with this skill. See the “Elemental Powers” section for details. Allies in your inspiring aura gain Armor 5 against attacks with the chosen element trait.

options.name: Vehicle
options.description: This emblem takes the form of a mechanical vehicle, such as a race-car, fire-truck, or fighter jet. You gain a Rank 1 Vehicle (see the “Vehicles” section for details). While transformed into your Hero form, you can summon your vehicle to you using an action. If you do so, it appears in the nearest unoccupied location to you that is large enough to fit the vehicle. Channeling the speed of a motorized vehicle, allies that start their turns in your aura gain +2 movement speed that turn.

options.name: Ancient Warriors
options.description:
This emblem takes the form of a legendary warrior from history, such as a Samurai, Knight, or Gladiator. You gain the following ability: Know your Enemy

Bolstered by the discipline of these ancient warriors, allies in your Inspiring Aura gain immunity to the charmed, frightened, and taunted conditions.

magical-guardian.name: Guardian Transformation
magical-guardian.description: As an action while holding your Guardian Accessory, you can speak your transformation phrase to transform into your Magical Guardian form. This transformation usually involves a costume change, the user being enveloped in bright light, and cool particle effects. When you activate your Guardian Transformation, increment your Power Die by 1. You also gain a number of “Charms” equal to your Primary Attribute. When an ally you can see makes a defense roll, you may choose to spend one “Charm” to add +1D to that ally’s roll (after seeing the result of their roll, but before determining the outcome). You can also spend an action to touch a willing creature and expend any number of “Charms” to heal that creature by 5 times the number of charms spent. Once you’ve transformed into your guardian form, you can’t do so again until you take a short rest.

magical-guardian.name: Guardian Accessory
magical-guardian.description: You have an important object - maybe an old family heirloom, personal treasure, or mysterious artifact given by a stranger. This object channels your energy, and allows you to transform.When you transform, your Accessory changes into one of the following forms.

options.name: Dazzling Wand
options.description: Your transformation item takes the form of a beautiful, ornate wand. You learn 1 additional Guardian Spell.

options.name: Shining Weapon
options.description: Choose a Rank 1 weapon - your Transformation Item takes the form of a glowing, ethereal weapon of that type. If you choose a Melee Weapon, you gain Beginner training in the Melee Weapons skill for that weapon. If you choose a Ranged Weapon, you gain Beginner training in Targeting for that weapon. Whichever option you chose, you gain a “Slow” progression in that skill.

options.name: Magnificent Gadget
options.description: Choose a skill you have at least Beginner training in. Your Transformation Object takes a form related to that skill. For example, if you choose the “Medicine” skill, it may transform into a Stethoscope. While holding the object, you have a +1D Item bonus to all rolls using that skill.

magical-guardian.name: Magical Familiar
magical-guardian.description: You have a magical familiar. This familiar may be a magical spirit who grants you powers, a pet who is transformed by your magical abilities, or any number of other whimsical beasts. Create a Rank 0 Familiar using the Familiar Subsystem.

metamorph.name: Metamorphic Transformations
metamorph.description: You know one of the following forms. You can choose to transform into your chosen form as an action. Each form has a number of unique adaptations, and you can choose which adaptations to activate when you transform. You may only have one of these forms active at a time. While transformed, you may spend an action to modify your chosen adaptations, or to switch into a different form (if you have multiple forms known).

options.name: Beast Form
options.description: You take on the properties of animals and beasts. When you transform into this form, choose 2 of the following adaptations: Gliding: You do not take damage from falling. For every square you fall, you may also move horizontally 1 square. Tough Hide: You gain 1 armor. Spikes: Your body is covered in dangerous spikes. Whenever an enemy deals Melee damage to you, they also take damage equal to 1 + your Metamorphosis Rank. Savage Claws: Your melee attacks deal additional damage equal to 1 + your Metamorphosis Rank. Enhanced Senses: You gain a +1D bonus to Perception, and you know the location of creatures within 10 squares of you (even if you cannot see them). Creatures you cannot see are Obscured but not Concealed. Amphibious: You can breathe both air and water, and gain a swim speed equal to your movement speed. Climber: You gain a climbing speed and can move freely on vertical surfaces. Burrower: You gain a burrowing speed equal to half your movement speed. Quadrupedal: You gain +2 Movement Speed Natural Camouflage: You gain a +1D Bonus to Stealth. You are Obscured to Ranged attacks.

options.name: Material Form
options.description: Your body takes on the properties of various materials. When you transform, choose 2 of the following adaptations: Elastic: Your melee attacks gain +1 reach. High Density: Whenever you would be pushed or pulled, reduce the distance by 2 squares. Magnetic: You can push and pull nearby metal objects. Metal weapons have a -1D penalty to attacks against you. If you hit a creature made of metal (or wearing metal armor) with an attack, you can push that creature a number of squares away from you equal to your Metamorphosis rank. Liquid: You can squeeze through small openings and cracks, and can share spaces with other creatures. Slippery: You cannot be immobilized. Sticky: When you succeed on a melee attack against a single target, you may choose to have that creature become grappled. Variable Hardness: When you take damage, gain 1 armor until the beginning of your next turn. If you take damage while you already have an armor bonus due to this ability, increase that bonus by 1.

options.name: Elemental Form
options.description: Your body takes on the properties of various elemental powers. Choose one of the following elements: Water, Fire, Earth, Air, Metal, Ice, Electricity. You gain the following traits for that element: Elemental Resistance: You gain Armor 5 against attacks with the chosen trait. Elemental Strikes: Your attacks gain the chosen trait, and have the “Attack Modifiers” of that element (see the Elemental Powers Subsystem).

metamorph.name: Monstrous Mimicry
metamorph.description: If you are in a square adjacent to a defeated enemy whose Threat Level is less than or equal to your Level, you may spend 1 action to absorb that enemy’s spirit energy. If you do so, you can temporarily transform into a copy of that enemy. This transformation lasts until the end of the combat, or until you spend an action to transform again.

monster-tamer.name: Monster Companion
monster-tamer.description: You have a Familiar - a creature formed from a strong bond and the power of your spirit energy. Use the Familiar Subsystem to determine the statistics for your familiar. You can choose any archetype and template, as well as 2 Rank 1 or lower Familiar Traits.

monster-tamer.name: Creature Summoning
monster-tamer.description: As an action, you can choose to summon or dismiss your bonded creature. When your creature is not summoned they may disappear to another dimension, or they may be stored in a physical object (such as an orb, figurine, or a card). Your creature disappears when it drops to 0 hit points, and it cannot be summoned again until you take a short rest.

monster-tamer.name: Basic Monster Taming
monster-tamer.description:
You gain the following technique, using your "Monster Taming" skill: Tame Monster 
You can also make “Investigate Enemy” actions using Intellect (Monster Taming). If you do so, you can ask the question “Can I tame this enemy”, in addition to the normal questions available to the “Investigate Enemy” action.

monster-tamer.name: Tamer Bond
monster-tamer.description: You have a Rank 1 Bond with your Monster Companion. This bond increases automatically as you level up, and is always equal to your Monster Taming skill Rank. If you have multiple Monster Companions, they are considered to have a bond of this same rank with each other. This bond also applies towards any Monsters that critically failed against your “Tame Monster” action.

ninja.name: Ninja Combat Training
ninja.description: Choose one of the following combat skills: Targeting, Melee Weapons, or Martial Arts. You have a Medium skill progression in the chosen skill. If you chose Targeting or Melee Weapons, you gain 1 Rank 1 weapon that uses that skill.

ninja.name: Exceptional Stealth
ninja.description: Your Stealth Skill is exceptional - you get Advanced training at level 1, and you are allowed to train the Stealth skill up to 1 rank higher than the standard skill rank cap.

ninja.name: Stealth Attack
ninja.description: If you make an attack against a single target while Concealed to that target, increase the degree of success by 1.

spirit-warrior.name: Reactive Strikes
spirit-warrior.description: When an enemy leaves your melee range, you can use a reaction to make a Martial Arts attack against that enemy. On a success, the target’s movement ends (in addition to the attack’s other effects).

spirit-warrior.name: Martial Arts Specialist
spirit-warrior.description: Choose one “Martial Arts Tag” from those listed in the Martial Arts section of the document. You gain a +1D specialization bonus when performing a technique with the chosen tag.

spirit-warrior.name: Combo System
spirit-warrior.description: Your mastery of Martial Arts allows you to string multiple techniques together to perform devastating combos. To perform a combo, you must declare all of the techniques you wish to perform, the targets of those techniques, and spend 3 actions and any required energy up-front. If you do so, the entire combo is treated as a single Activity, and therefore does not suffer from any repeated action penalties. A combo must start with an “Opener”, can contain any number of “Follow-Up”s, and may end with a “Finisher”. It is not required for a combo to contain all three types of moves - the only restrictions are that it must begin with an Opener, and if a finisher is used it must be at the end of the combo. At level 1, if you choose to perform a combo you must spend all 3 actions to do so.

spirit-warrior.name: Warrior’s Mark
spirit-warrior.description: When you hit an enemy with a melee attack, you can choose to mark that enemy as your chosen target. Only 1 enemy may be marked this way at a time, and you cannot switch targets until the current target is knocked out or surrenders. While an enemy is marked in this way, both you and the enemy have a -1D status penalty to attack anyone besides the other. Also, you may make a reactive strike against the marked enemy if they attack a target other than you.

spirit-warrior.name: Stances
spirit-warrior.description: Your character knows 2 of the following Stances.. Each stance grants a passive benefit, as well as a drawback. Entering a stance, or switching between stances, requires an action.

options.name: Tortoise Stance
options.description:
Emulate the steadfastness of the tortoise - slow but sturdy.
Passive: Gain +1D Status Bonus to all defenses. If you would be pushed or pulled by an enemy ability, reduce the number of squares of movement by your Martial Arts rank. Drawback: Your Speed is halved, and you take a -1D status penalty to attacks.

options.name: Tiger Stance
options.description: Strike with the single-minded ferocity of a hunting tiger. Passive: Add +2 damage to all unarmed strikes Drawback: When you enter this stance you gain the “off guard 1” condition, and this condition cannot be cleared as long as you are in this stance.

options.name: Shark Stance
options.description: Channel the surging speed of a shark, endlessly chasing its prey. Passive: Increase your movement speed by 2. You gain a +1D circumstance bonus to dodge rolls. Drawback: You cannot use “grapple” techniques, and must use at least half of your movement each turn, if possible.

options.name: Crane Stance
options.description: Soar and gently float, like a majestic but fragile crane. Passive: You may expend your movement to perform incredible leaps. You may leap to a maximum height equal to half your movement (rounded down). While in this stance, you do not take fall damage. Drawback: You have a -1D penalty to resist being pushed, pulled, or grappled. When you would be pushed or pulled, you are moved 1 additional square.

options.name: Python Stance
options.description: Restrain and crush your enemies like a powerful constrictor snake. Passive: You gain a +1D status bonus to techniques with the “grapple” tag, you can grapple creatures up to 2 scales larger than you, and moving while grappling does not cost extra movement. Drawback: You cannot use techniques with the “aerial” or “mobile” tags.

options.name: Gorilla Stance
options.description: Like a cornered silverback, you lash out against multiple enemies surrounding you. Passive: When you perform a technique with the “flurry” or “push” tags, you may perform 1 additional unarmed strike or shove on a different target. Your Martial Arts rank is treated as 1 lower for the purpose of this additional attack. Drawback: You cannot mark enemies, and all current marks end.

weapon-master.name: Soulbound Weapon
weapon-master.description:
You have a special connection with a particular weapon, and can easily channel your spirit energy through it. You gain a level 1 weapon of your choice. You gain a Specialization in the chosen weapon type (+1D Specialization Bonus). The weapon gains the “soulbound” enhancement (This enhancement does not count towards your weapon’s enhancement slots). Its rank automatically increases, and is always equal to the rank of the skill used to attack with this weapon.

Soulbound: As an action, you can cause the weapon to teleport from its current location and materialize in your hands, or disappear into a higher-dimensional space. This enhancement CANNOT be removed or exchanged by abilities that allow you to modify your weapon’s enhancements. A soulbound weapon does not take up a weapon slot.

weapon-master.name: Weapon Enhancement
weapon-master.description: Your soulbound weapon is no ordinary weapon. Choose one additional Level 1 weapon enhancement (from the “Weapon” section) to apply to your Soulbound Weapon.

weapon-master.name: Fighting Styles
weapon-master.description: Choose one of the following fighting styles. Each style has a prerequisite, based on your chosen Soulbound weapon. Each fighting style provides a passive buff to weapons of that type, and a recommended technique that fits the style.

options.name: Sentinel Style
options.description: Prerequisite: Soulbound Weapon with 'Reach 2+' tag. Sweet-spot : When you hit a creature that is not adjacent to you with a melee attack using a weapon with the 'Reach 2+' tag, you deal an additional amount of damage equal to 2 times your Melee Weapons Skill Rank. Recommended Technique: Keep At Bay

options.name: Paired Weapons Style
options.description: Prerequisites: Soulbound weapon with the “One-handed” tag Two-point defense: You gain access to an additional Rank 1 weapon with the one-handed trait. While wielding a one-handed weapon in both hands, Treat the number of enemies adjacent to you as one fewer for the purposes of flanking. Techniques: Dual-Strike

options.name: Barrage Style
options.description: Prerequisites: Soulbound Weapon with the 'Thrown' tag. Passive Benefit: While wielding a thrown weapon, all thrown weapons you wield gain the 'Returning' tag for as long as you wield them and have this fighting style. Recommended Technique: Spread Volley

options.name: Marksman Style
options.description: Prerequisites: Soulbound Weapon with the 'Ranged' tag. Sharpshooter’s aim: When you take the “Aim” action, also double the range of your ranged weapon attacks. Recommended Technique: Piercing Shot

options.name: Skirmisher Style
options.description: Prerequisites: Soulbound Weapon with the 'Ranged' or ‘Thrown’ tag. Point-Blank Proficiency: You do not provoke reactions from creatures when making a ranged or thrown attack while adjacent to them. You also do not take a -1D penalty to these attacks while adjacent to a hostile creature. Recommended Technique: Slowing Shot

options.name: Titan Style
options.description: Prerequisites: Soulbound Weapon with the “Heavy” or “Two-Handed” tag. Armor Breaker: When you deal a critical success to a creature with a melee attack using a weapon with the 'Heavy' tag, that creature suffers a -1D penalty to their Physical Defense until the end of their next turn. Recommended Technique: Devastating Blow

options.name: Guardian Style
options.description: Prerequisite: Soulbound Weapon with the 'Defensive' tag Vigilant Defense: When you use the “Parry” reaction, you gain a +1D bonus to all other defense rolls until the end of your next turn. Recommended Technique: Protective Parry

options.name: Smasher Style
options.description: Prerequisite: Soulbound Weapon with the 'Blunt' tag. Bash: Your blunt weapons gain the “Bash” Alternative Use: Alternative Use - Bash: You swing your weapon to knock away your enemies. On a failure, push the target back 1 square. On a success, push the target 1 additional square for every hit that exceeds their Defense. Recommended Technique: Armor Crusher

options.name: Blademaster Style
options.description: Prerequisite: Soulbound weapon with the 'Sharp' tag. Exploit Vulnerability: Bleeding enemies have a -1D penalty to defenses against your attacks Recommended Technique: Vicious Rend

weapon-master.name: Heart Stealer
weapon-master.description: Whenever you drop an enemy creature to 0 hit points, you regain a number of hit points equal to your Strength + Your Level.

feats.name: Additional Emblem
feats.description: Choose an additional Level 1 Emblem from the list. When you transform, you may choose which emblem to use (only one emblem is active at a time). While transformed, you may choose to switch emblems as an action.

feats.name: Chroma Ranger Initiate
feats.description: Chroma Armor: When you transform, your armor takes on a single main hue. Choose a color below. You gain +5 armor against certain attack tags based on the color chosen. Red: Fire Blue: Water Green: Poison Black: Earth Pink: Wind Yellow: Electric White: Ice/Cold Fight as One: You gain a number of “Boosts” equal to your Primary Attribute. When you or an ally in your aura succeeds on an attack roll, you can spend 1 “Boost” to Mark one of the targets. All attacks against the marked target get a +1D Status bonus until the end of your next turn.

feats.name: Explosive Transformation
feats.description: Explosive Transformation (Free Reaction): Primary (Henshin Arts) vs Spiritual Defense. Range: 1 Square Burst. Your transformation unleashes a huge amount of energy, which you can channel into an explosion of energy around you. Damage: 5. On a critical success, targets are also Dazzled.

feats.name: Giant of Light Initiate
feats.description: Giant of Light Form: While in Hero Form, as an action you may choose to gain 1 strain and transform into a Giant of Light form. When you do, increment your power die. Your scale increases by 1, and you gain +1 armor (stacking with your Hero form’s armor). While in this form you gain +1 strain at the beginning of each of your turns. Once you activate this form, it cannot be activated again until you take a short rest.

feats.name: Masked Rider Initiate
feats.description: TBD
feats.grantsNotes: Intended feat listed under Henshin Hero Feats Level 2 in the handbook.

feats.name: Animal Transformation
feats.description: When you activate your Guardian Transformation, or as an action while transformed, you can enter an animal form. You may choose any Scale 0 or 1 animal that does not have a fly speed or a swim speed. You maintain your mental and spiritual attributes, but your physical attributes match your new form. You retain access to your skills and Powers, but can only perform actions that your new form is capable of.

feats.name: Celestial Knight Path Initiate
feats.description: Celestial Armor: While transformed, your Guardian form gains a suit of magical armor. This armor grants you a +1D Equipment bonus to all defenses while adjacent to an enemy. Knight Combat Training: You do not suffer a penalty when making a ranged Spell attack while within 5 feet of an enemy. You gain Beginner Training and a Slow skill progression in either Targeting or Melee Weapons.

feats.name: Dark Witch Path Initiate
feats.description: Fallen Rose Necklace: While in Guardian Form, as an action you may choose to gain 1 strain and transform into a Witch form. When you do, increment your power die. Additional witch form benefits TBD. While in this form you gain +1 strain at the beginning of each of your turns. If you become overstrained while in this form, roll on the “Curse Table” below. The curse lasts until the end of combat. Once you activate this form, it cannot be activated again until you take a short rest. Design note: I might have to rearrange things a bit, but maybe while in Witch form, every time you successfully hit an enemy with an attack the *enemy* becomes cursed, and rolls on the curse table?

feats.name: Dazzling Transformation
feats.description: Your transformation sequence is so bright and sparkling that it has the potential to dazzle your enemies. When you transform, roll Spellcasting vs Spiritual Defense, affecting all hostile creatures within 4 squares. On a failed defense, those creatures are Off-Guard 1 , or Off-Guard 2 on a critical failure.

feats.name: Merfolk Transformation
feats.description: When you activate your Guardian Transformation, or as an action while transformed, you can activate a Merfolk form. In this form, your legs transform into a fish or dolphin-like tail, you gain the ability to breathe both air and water, and you can swim at a speed equal to your walking speed. Your movement on land is halved in this form, and you cannot jump.

feats.name: Sacred Priestex Staff
feats.description: Your Transformation Object begins to shine with Sacred Spiritual Energy. It has a special store of Energy (separate from your character’s energy) equal to your Spellcasting Attribute, and regains 1 Energy per round. This energy can only be spent on spells to heal an ally, or spells that target a Spirit.

feats.name: Sparkling Idol Path Initiate
feats.description: Sparkling Melody Instrument: This accessory can take the form of any musical instrument, including a microphone. You gain the Idol Inspiration Reaction. Idol Inspiration (1 Reaction): When an ally within 6 squares fails a skill roll, you can inspire them with a quick song. They roll a number of additional dice equal to your Spellcasting Rank. Design note: Work-in-progress

feats.name: Spell Sniper
feats.description: Double the range of all of your attack spells that have a range of at least 2 squares. Enemies do not gain a defense bonus due to being obscured against your spells.

feats.name: Instant Transformation
feats.description: When you roll initiative you can automatically transform into your Magical Guardian form (no action required).

feats.name: Titan Shifter Path Initiate
feats.description:
Titan Form: (1 Action)
Increment your power die. Your scale increases by 1. Your Titan Form can stack with your Metamorphic Transformations. This form lasts until the end of the combat, or until you choose to end it (no action required). Once you activate your Titan Form, you cannot do so again until you take a short rest.

feats.name: Monster Fusion
feats.description: Choose two of your Monster Companions. If both creatures are summoned you may spend 1 action and X energy to fuse them into a single creature.

feats.name: Multiple Simultaneous Summons
feats.description: Higher level feat. Allows summoning two Monster Companions at once.

feats.name: Combined Attack
feats.description: Player and Creature both attack the same enemy, granting a bonus

feats.name: Gadget School
feats.description: Ability to improve using gadgets. Gadget ideas - Smoke Bombs, Grappling Hook, Weapon Launchers, Firecrackers / Explosives, Poisons

feats.name: Illusion School
feats.description: You can generate minor visual illusions. As an action, you can expend 1 energy to create a simple, immobile visual illusion that does not exceed 5 feet in any dimension. You can only have one such illusion active at a time. In addition, you gain +2 to detect or resist visual, auditory, and mental illusions.

feats.name: Shadow School
feats.description: Improves ability to hide in shadows, maybe teleport from shadow to shadow

feats.name: Summoning School
feats.description: You gain a Rank 1 Familiar (See Familiar Subsystem).

feats.name: Dizzy Fist Initiate
feats.description: Your erratic, swaying movements make you an unpredictable opponent. When you get a failure on an attack against an enemy, they gain a +1D circumstance penalty to their defenses against your next attack. After performing one attack in a combo, you can choose to swap out the next technique in the combo, or change the target of the technique. Once per round, when an enemy misses you with an attack, you can shift 1 square (no action required)

feats.name: Qi Blaster Initiate
feats.description: This path grants additional control over the spiritual energy that courses through your body. Reduce the energy cost of Martial Arts techniques with the “Spirit” tag by 1. When you perform the “Spirit Blast” technique, you can spend additional energy to modify the range and shape of the attack. 3 energy - Instead of a single target, the attack deals damage to creatures in a 3 square cone or a 6 square line. Note: Energy spent in this way does not increase the damage of the attack

feats.name: Soulrager Initiate
feats.description: The power inside of you is more than you can control, so most of the time it stays locked down. However, in moments of intense danger the power can come bursting out. As an action you can choose to gain 1 strain and transform into your Soulrager form. When you activate your Soulrager Transformation, increment your Power Die by 1. You gain armor equal to your Martial Arts rank, and your unarmed strikes also deal increased damage equal to this amount. At the beginning of each of your turns, you gain 1 strain while in this form. Whenever you exit this form, or become overstrained while in this form, you take damage equal to your current strain. Once you activate this form, it cannot be activated again until you take a short rest.

feats.name: Spirit Manipulator Initiate
feats.description: This Path can not only manipulate the Spirit Energy in themselves, but also in others. Once per round, when you roll a success on a Martial Arts attack against a marked enemy, you may reduce that enemy’s Energy by 1, and increment your Power Die. As an action, you may also spend energy to restore hit points to yourself or one adjacent ally. You restore HP equal to 2 * the amount of energy spent.

feats.name: Split Focus
feats.description: You are experienced at fighting multiple opponents at once. You may mark up to 2 enemies simultaneously, and do not take a -1D penalty for attacking enemies that are not marked. When you perform a Martial Arts technique that allows more than one strike, you may perform those strikes against different targets and may move between strikes.

feats.name: Stance Adept
feats.description: Gain an additional Rank 1 Stance. When you roll initiative, you may immediately activate a stance (no action required).

feats.name: What Doesn’t Kill You
feats.description: Once per combat, when your health would drop to 0, instead of becoming defeated you regain HP equal to 2 * Strength, and increment your Power Die.

feats.name: Commander Path Initiate
feats.description: This path focuses on support and leadership.You gain the following benefits: Shared Heart: When your Heart Stealer feature activates, instead of restoring HP to yourself you can choose to restore HP to one ally you can see that you share a bond with. Tactical Order: You gain a number of “Commands” equal to your Heart attribute. When an ally you can see makes a attack roll, you may choose to spend one “Command” to add +1D to that ally’s roll (after seeing the result of their roll, but before determining the outcome). You may also spend 1 action and 1 command to grant 1 additional action to an ally you can see.

feats.name: Energy Reservoir
feats.description: Your Soulbound weapon serves as a reserve of energy, and can store an amount equal to your Attunement. As an action you can retrieve this energy from your weapon, increasing your own pool of available energy. The weapon’s reserves recharge when you take a short rest.

feats.name: Ghostwalker Path Initiate
feats.description: You are able make your body semi-incorporeal, allowing you to (and your weapon) to temporarily phase through obstacles. When you move, you can move through objects and enemies (but cannot end your turn in the same space as another creature or object). You know the locations of creatures behind full cover, and they are only obscured (but not concealed) from you. When you make an attack, you may spend 3 energy to ignore cover for that attack, as your weapon phases through the obstacle.

feats.name: Soul Stealer
feats.description: Once per round when you trigger Heart Stealer, instead of healing yourself you may choose to increment your Power die instead. If you defeat a creature whose threat level is equal to or higher than your level, you may instead choose to regain 1 action point. Possible Feats / Class Features (Uncategorized)

feats.name: Soulbound Pair
feats.description: You gain a second one-handed soulbound weapon. Both weapons are treated as a single weapon - they can both be summoned as part of the same action, and any weapon enhancements on your soulbound weapon apply to both (as long as they meet the prerequisites).

feats.name: Warden Soul Path Initiate
feats.description: Your soulbound weapon is able to manifest as a visible, but incorporeal, spirit. It is treated as a Rank 1 familiar with the “Spirit” template. You also gain the following benefits: Soulguard Defender: Your weapon spirit helps defend you against spiritual attacks. You gain armor 1 vs Spiritual Defense Rolls. Your Weapons with the “Defensive” tag can parry attacks that target Spiritual Defense, as well as Physical Defense.

feats.name: Weaponsmith
feats.description: Choose an additional Level 1 weapon enhancement, and apply it to one of your weapons. This enhancement does not count towards that weapon’s enhancement slots. When you take a short rest, you can change this enhancement (either replacing it with a different enhancement, or moving it to a new weapon).

origins.name: Artifact
origins.summary: A semi-sentient magical or technological relic chose you and now grants your powers.
origins.description: A powerful magical / technological item grants the heroes their powers. This item is very explicitly NOT created by the character - instead, they were given, chosen by, or simply found the item. The item has a long and mysterious history, which is unknown to the user at first. The artifact can take many forms, but is always at least semi-sentient, and has a will of its own. It has chosen its wielder for a purpose, but they must discover what that purpose is.

features.name: Bonded Relic
features.description: The artifact is not just an object - it has a will of its own, and a unique relationship with its wielder. You gain a Rank 2 bond with the Artifact. This relationship is a two-way street - the Artifact grants you power, but also expects something of you in return. Determine what the Artifact’s goal is, and summarize it in a short statement - a sentence or two. Whenever you perform an action that helps the Artifact further its goal, increment your Power Die.

features.name: Living Archive
features.description: The Artifact grants its wielder more than just power - it can share a wealth of knowledge as well . This knowledge may be innate, or it may have been accumulated over time from the collective knowledge of its past wielders. Either way, the Artifact can allow its wielder to occasionally tap into this knowledge. The Artifact starts out knowing 3 Skills. When its wielder makes a skill check using one of those known skills, they can spend 2 energy to allow the Artifact to “Assist” them on the action.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: What type of object is the Artifact? What is unique about its appearance, compared to other artifacts of that type?
What does your character know about the origins / history of the artifact?
Who else might want to take the artifact’s power for themselves?
What are the artifact’s goals? What is it trying to accomplish?
Why did it choose your character as a wielder? What qualities was it drawn to?
How does the artifact communicate with you? Can it speak telepathically, does it beam fragments of images into your mind, or are its methods subtler?
What is your relationship with the artifact - are you a willing follower, grudging accomplice, loyal partner, or scheming to bend the artifact’s power to your own will?
higherLevelUpgrades: Artifact dimension - entering a micro-dimension that exists inside the artifact itself
Voices of the past - ability to speak with past wielders of the artifact (grants additional skill bonuses)?

origins.name: Inventor
origins.summary: Your powers come from technology and inventions that you built yourself.
origins.description: The character created machines / technology that manipulates energy for them, granting them special powers. This character does not actually have “Powers” per-se, but can mimic their effects with their technology.

features.name: Powerful Inventions
features.description:
Your character’s “Powers” come from their inventions. This means that they have a physical form, and take up physical space. Your character can only use their powers when they are “equipped”, and they take up space in their inventory. Your powers gain the “Technology” tag, and lose the “Magic” tag (if any).

Design Note: Need to implement an inventory / equipment system. Should be relatively simple and streamlined.

features.name: Flexible Gadgets
features.description: You have invented a number of additional utility gadgets equal to your Intellect. You can carry as many of these inventions with you as you wish, but each one takes up a spot in your inventory, and you may only activate one gadget at a time. While activated, each gadget grants a +1D equipment bonus to a single Utility Skill that you have at least Rank 1 training in. You may switch your activated gadget with another in your inventory as an action.

features.name: Empowering Tech
features.description: Once per round, when you make a Skill roll with an equipment bonus, you may increment your Power Die.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers
roleplayQuestions: How did your character learn to create this technology?
Who wants to get their hands on your character’s inventions, or use them for their own purposes?
What “opus” does your character hope to one day invent?
Where does your character create their inventions?
Where are the inventions stored when not in use?
How does your character carry their inventions with them?
What physical form does your character's “Powers” take?
higherLevelUpgrades: More potent inventions (+2D equipment bonus)
Shared Inventions (Other characters may activate and equip gadgets)
Power Bank - A limited battery of Spirit Energy that can be used to power abilities
Microverse Backpack - inventions do not take up inventory space, stored in pocked dimension
Portable workshop - Upgrade to Microverse Backpack - can carry an entire workshop in a pocket dimension
Quick-Swap - Switching inventions once per turn does not require an action

origins.name: Spiritual Bloodline
origins.summary: Your family line carries an innate connection to spirit energy and long-standing spiritual traditions.
origins.description: You come from a bloodline with an innate and powerful connection to the flow of spiritual energy. Thanks to your family's special sensory abilities you can sense the flow of spirit energy and see spirit phenomena that are normally invisible to others. You have heard legends of ancestors that were able to perform incredible feats of spiritual power, but you are still just beginning to scratch the surface of the potential that lies within you. However, along with these incredible gifts, you also carry the incredible burden of familial expectations and traditions.

features.name: Eyes of Legend
features.description:
One of your bloodline’s most foundational techniques is the ability to channel spiritual energy into their eyes. On your turn, you may spend 1 Energy to activate your eye technique, allowing you to see invisible creatures by sensing their spiritual energy. You can also see other traces of concentrated spiritual energy, such as infused objects or the remnants of recent spiritual activity.
Activating this ability triggers an obvious physical change in your eyes’ appearance. They may begin to glow, change color, your pupil might change shape, or some other obvious physical transformation.

features.name: Soul Reader
features.description: You may perform Investigation Actions using Attunement instead of Intellect. Once per round, when you act upon knowledge learned from an Investigation Action, you may increment your Power die.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: Who are some of your famous ancestors, and what are they known for? Are they legends among the general public, or spoken of in whispers among select groups?
What role do you play in the family? Are you an heir to the main branch, destined to one day lead? Or are you a low-ranking member of a side-branch, forever overshadowed by your higher-born cousins?
What traditions and expectations does your family have? Do they enforce a rigid code of conduct, or perform strange rituals?
How common are powers among your family? Is it a foregone conclusion that every member will be born with powers, or is it less common?
How does your bloodline relate to your powers? Does it always show up the same way (for example, an ancient Ninja clan), or does everyone develop their powers differently?
higherLevelUpgrades: Innate Spiritual “Armor” (reduce damage from spiritual attacks)
Spiritual Form - You gain the ability to project your spirit energy outside of your body. Your powers are limited in this form, but it does allow you to explore and gather information undetected.
More powerful “eyes” that allow you to detect and predict an enemy’s moves. Enhances dodge action?
Spiritual Link - See through the eyes of another?
Ability to hide / suppress your own spiritual energy (for stealth)?

origins.name: Soul Vessel
origins.summary: A powerful entity lives within you, and you draw on its strength at great risk.
origins.description: Your power comes from another spirit or being that lives within you. This relationship is mutualistically symbiotic - you rely on the entity for power, and it relies on you for survival. However, that doesn’t necessarily make it friendly - the entities are often malevolent, and don’t necessarily take kindly to having to share their power.

features.name: Font of Power
features.description:
The entity’s spiritual energy is enormous, and can be drawn upon to enhance your own abilities. However, calling upon such power is not without cost. At any time, you may choose to take 1 Strain and gain a number of energy equal to your Willpower.
Once per round, when you take Strain, you may increment your Power Die.

features.name: Lurking Threat
features.description:
The entity inside you doesn’t enjoy being restrained, and is always looking for an opportunity to break free. When you drop to 0 HP or become Overstrained, the entity seizes the opportunity and temporarily possesses you, gaining control of your body and activating an “unleashed” transformation. During this transformation your appearance changes to more closely resemble the Entity’s true form.
When you enter this form you regain all of your HP. While transformed, you must attempt to deal as much total damage to other creatures as possible, and make no distinction between allies and enemies. Any time you would deal damage to a creature, deal additional damage equal to your Willpower. This form ends when you drop to 0 HP again or combat ends.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: Who is the spirit trapped inside you (alien entity, powerful spirit, etc)?
How did the spirit become trapped, and who trapped it?
Why were you chosen as the vessel?
What would it take for the entity to escape?
What threat does the entity pose if it were to get free?
higherLevelUpgrades: Being able to pull on more of the entity’s power, with less risk
Being able to intentionally trigger + control the berserk form
Forming a bond with the entity, granting “assist” or “teamwork” actions
Fully transforming into the entity’s true form
A “combined form”, that grants full control and access to the entity’s powers

origins.name: Mutation
origins.summary: Your powers come from your altered biology and rapid adaptation to the world around you.
origins.description: You are the next step in genetic evolution, and your powers are rooted in your unique biology. In addition to granting you your abilities, your mutation makes you more able to adapt to your environment and circumstances. In mere moments, you can adapt to situations that would normally take evolution millions of years to overcome. However, these abilities come at a cost - your physical adaptations clearly mark you as “other”, and can make it more challenging to fit in with society.

features.name: Exploratory Adaptations
features.description: Your mutations can be triggered by environmental factors, granting you a number of beneficial adaptations. You can activate these adaptations by spending 1 action and 2 energy. You may only have one adaptation active at a time.

features.name: Amphibious Form
features.description: Your shape changes to be more suited to navigating the water - your hands and feet become webbed, you sprout gills, and you may gain other traits of aquatic / semi-aquatic creatures. While in this form you can breathe both air and water, and can swim at your normal movement speed.

features.name: Wall Crawler
features.description: You gain the ability to cling to vertical surfaces, allowing you to climb at full speed. You can climb even smooth surfaces and ceilings, as long as they are not wet or slippery.

features.name: Camouflage
features.description: Your skin adapts to blend in with your environment. You gain a +1 Status Bonus to Stealth checks.

features.name: Defensive Evolution
features.description:
Your mutation also helps protect you from external threats. When you take damage, you may spend 2 energy to adapt to that attack. If you do, choose 1 of that attack’s “tags” - you gain Armor (e.g. reduce damage) from attacks with the chosen tag equal to your Strength. For example, if you have Strength 3 and are hit with a fire-based attack, you can gain heat-resistant skin that grants Armor 3 to subsequent “fire” attacks. Lasts until the end of this combat.
Once per round when you reduce damage due to Armor, you may increment your Power Die.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: What unusual physical traits does your mutation grant you? Are the changes to your appearance subtle (like strangely shaped pupils), or extremely obvious (your entire body is covered in shimmering scales)?
Was your mutation obvious from birth, or did it appear later in life?
Who has mistreated you in the past due to your mutations? Who has shown you empathy?
How do you feel about your mutations - do you see them as more of a gift, or a curse?
Do you try to hide your unusual appearance, or display it proudly?
higherLevelUpgrades: Temporary Gliding / Flight
“Natural Armor” - increased physical defenses
“Natural Weapons” - spikes, claws, teeth
Size changing?
Enhanced senses?

origins.name: Getaba Warrior
origins.summary: You are a disciplined alien warrior from a predatory, militaristic species.
origins.description: You are a member of the Getaba Species - a society that is built on a foundation of military prowess. Getaba warriors are some of the most powerful, disciplined warriors in the galaxy. Most of the time they appear mostly humanoid, but with some features that make them somewhat resemble a predatory animal - sharp teeth, slitted pupils, sometimes sharp pointed ears, and a tail. Getaba come in several varieties - some have features resembling Earth Wolves, while others are more akin to big cats such as Leopards or Tigers, or any number of other predatory beasts. When Getaba enter battle these features can become even more prominent, and some Getaba can even transform into a fully bestial form.

features.name: Hunter’s Instinct
features.description:
A skilled hunter chooses their prey carefully. You may make an investigation action against a group of enemies, rather than an individual. Examples of questions include:
* Which enemy has the highest (or lowest) HP?
* Which enemy has the weakest physical (or mental, or spiritual) defense?
* Which enemy is the highest Threat Level?
* Which enemy is the most wounded?
* Which enemy has the highest Strength (or Agility)?
As usual for Investigation Actions, you gain a +1D Circumstance bonus when acting upon this information

features.name: Thrill of Battle
features.description: Once per round, when you take damage from a melee attack, you may increment your Power Die.

features.name: Keen Senses
features.description: Your senses of hearing and smell are particularly sensitive. Using these powerful senses, you can detect the presence of hidden non-spirit creatures within 60 feet of you. You know their location, and they are only considered to be “obscured”, rather than “concealed”. In addition, if you know the scent of a particular creature or object, you can detect if they were in a particular location within the last hour, and if they are within 1000 feet you can detect which direction they are in.

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: What is your relationship with the Getaba army - are you a loyal member, a deserter, or were you raised apart from Getaba society?
What type of predatory animal do you take traits from - a wolf, big cat, bear, or something more obscure like a Badger or even an Orca?
What is your opinion of other species (such as Humans) - do you try to understand them, or do you see them as nothing more than the next conquest?
How do you feel about your predatory instincts - do you suppress them, or embrace them?
higherLevelUpgrades: Transformations (Getaba elite, beast mode)
Elite military training - improvements to flanking, teamwork
“War Cry” ability?

origins.name: Powerful Patron
origins.summary: A stronger being lends you power, influence, and expectations you are meant to fulfill.
origins.description: You get your powers from another, more powerful being - whether an ancient spirit, a powerful energy-user, or extradimensional being. This Patron grants you access to a portion of their power, but they expect something from you in return. Your patron is not just powerful spiritually - they also have influence, resources, and connections that you can occasionally tap into to help you accomplish their goals.

features.name: Bond of Patronage
features.description: You gain a level 1 Bond with your Patron. Your Patron has certain expectations of you. If you fail to live up to your side of the bargain, the bond can be “Broken”. If this happens, your Patron can choose to restrict access to your powers, or even temporarily prevent you from accessing them entirely.

features.name: Irresistible Authority
features.description:
Your patron’s influence is powerful and widespread. With the mere mention of their name, other creatures become more willing to help (or fear) you. When you perform an action with the “Emotion” trait, you can spend a number of energy up to your Bond Rank with your Patron. Add a status bonus to the roll equal to the amount of energy spent.
Your Patron’s authority over you also helps shield you from other, lesser influences. You can also gain this Status Bonus when making a defense check against an effect with the “Emotion” trait.

features.name: Friends in High Places
features.description:
When making a skill check for resources or connections, your Patron may “Assist” you with the roll.

Design Note: As of writing this, I have not written a system that requires making rolls for resources or connections. However, I plan to eventually implement a system for these types of checks. This system will cover things like “can I afford item X” (instead of tracking money), “Is Item Y available in this city”, “Can I find information on Z”, or “Do I know anyone who ____?”

origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: Who is your patron? What kind of being are they, what kind of power and influence do they have?
How did you meet your Patron?
Why did you form a bond with your Patron?
What are your Patron’s goals, and how do they expect you to help achieve them?
What kind of behavior does your Patron expect of you? What actions do they require, or prohibit?
What is your relationship like with your Patron. Do you agree with their goals, or are you just going along with them for Power?
higherLevelUpgrades: Ability to summon the Patron to temporarily fight along-side you?

Maybe abilities shouldn’t scale automatically with Bond Level - they should require feats to get stronger? Or maybe you can’t improve bond level normally - higher level upgrades might be required to increase bond level?
Defensive power - Ward around self or allies
Emotion abilities affect multiple nearby targets?

origins.name: Slimefolk
origins.summary: A slime-based origin exists in the handbook outline, but the current source text is incomplete.

This Patron grants you access to a portion of their power, but they expect something from you in return. origins.name: Magical Dimension
origins.summary: You came to this world from another realm where supernatural powers are normal.
origins.description:
You have recently arrived in this world from another. In your homeworld, magical powers are commonplace - a far cry from the mundane, modern world you now find yourself in. Although you are no longer in your homeworld, you retain your supernatural abilities.

Design Note: The following origins are more conceptual, and have not been implemented (and will not be implemented in Version 2.0)

Design Note: Features TBD
origins.originKeystone: You gain one Keystone that describes the origin of your character’s powers.
roleplayQuestions: Are your powers innate / biological, or learned? Are similar abilities commonplace where you come from, or are you unusual even among your own people?
Which time / place / dimension are you from? How did you end up here?

Not from around here - because you come from a different realm, the world you now find yourself in is very unfamiliar to you. You may take penalties when your ignorance of the culture or technology of this new world causes problems for you.

Part of you still belongs where you came from. You can serve as a conduit to open a portal to that time / realm (this still requires a power or ability to create a portal, you just serve as a focus for the destination).
Maybe at high levels you gain the ability to travel back and forth even more easily, or create the portals yourself?
You have knowledge and connections in your home-realm

origins.name: Curse
origins.summary: Your power is the product of a curse or malicious magic that grants strength at a cost.
origins.description: Your powers are granted by a curse, hex, or other malicious magic. Although your powers seem like a gift, they come with a cost.
roleplayQuestions: Who cursed you? Why? What do they want?
Curse should be tied to a character’s past - something they did caused them to be cursed. What is it?
How can the curse be broken? Does your character want to break it?
What is the cost of the curse? Is the cost something that has already happened, or something that will happen?

Is there a drawback to using the powers that the curse grants you? Curse is tied to a vice - using the powers could corrupt the character making them more likely to fall to the temptation of their vice?

origins.name: Manifest Spirit
origins.summary: Your subconscious power takes shape as a separate entity tied to your emotions.
origins.description: You cannot control your power directly - rather, it is controlled by your subconscious, and manifests as a separate entity.
roleplayQuestions: Emotional link - because the power is subconscious, it is strongly affected by your emotions. Choose an emotion that strengthens the spirit, and a different emotion that weakens it.

Tethered Spirit - the spirit can move and use abilities on its own (still requiring your actions and energy to use). It gets weaker the further it strays from your location. Any damage the spirit takes is passed along to you.
What form does this spirit take? How does this reflect or react to your emotions?
How did your character first manifest this spirit? (Moment of extremely strong emotion)
Ultimate Move - Behind the Mask - The spirit undergoes a transformation, unlocking greater power. This is connected with a moment of clarity or emotional growth of the character

origins.name: Demon of Hard Work
origins.summary: Your power comes from extreme training, discipline, and self-imposed limits rather than magic or technology.
origins.description: Your powers are not biological, mechanical, magical, or spiritual. You simply learned to enhance your own body, and harness the energy that surrounds you, through sheer hard work.
roleplayQuestions: Training is important - downtime requirement?
What is your motivation - why do you work so hard?

Ultimate Move - Limiter Removal - You always hold yourself back in some way - whether that be carrying around extra weight, wearing physical restraints, or a device that limits your energy consumption. Using your full potential puts a huge strain on your body, but you can do it for short periods of time.

origins.name: Legend Reborn
origins.summary: You are the reincarnation of an earlier hero and are only beginning to access their legacy.
origins.description: You are a reincarnation of a hero from the past. You inherit their powers, and perhaps some of their memories, but are still learning how to access them. You were reincarnated in this time and place for a reason - there is an evil from your past that has risen again, and it is up to you to stop them once again.

origins.name: Experiment
origins.summary: Your powers were forced on you through a dangerous scientific, governmental, or spiritual experiment.
origins.description:
Your powers are man-made - granted by a scientific or government experiment in which you were an unwilling participant. These powers were triggered by dangerous chemical, biological, or spiritual manipulations, and many of the experimental subjects didn’t survive. You are one of the rare successes, and the organization that performed the experiments will do anything to learn how to repeat the outcome.

Design Note: Most of the “Core” Origins assume that the character is a human. For future “supplements” that explore settings beyond Earth X (E.g. Isekai World, Galactic Empire), characters will have more options that go beyond this assumption. Isekai World will have more “Classic Fantasy” races with a JRPG / Isekai Twist (Elves, Orcs, Slimes, Demi-humans). There might be a “Spirit World” expansion with more spiritual origins - “Kami”, “Kitsune”, “Yokai”, etc. The Galactic Empire Expansion might have more alien species as Origin options (e.g. “Celeston”, Droid / mechanical being)

weaponEnhancements.description: Choose Water, Fire, Earth, or Wind; attacks made with the weapon gain that elemental tag and its related effects.
weaponEnhancements.notes: See Elemental Subsystem for exact effects.

weaponEnhancements.name: Extended Reach
weaponEnhancements.description: A melee weapon's attack range increases beyond normal melee reach.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Increase the weapon's reach by 1 square; use normalized reach wording in export/display.

weaponEnhancements.name: Enhanced Targeting
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is easier to line up for careful shots.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Gain an additional +1D status bonus when taking the Aim action.

weaponEnhancements.name: Agile Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is balanced for speed and finesse rather than brute force.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Attack rolls and damage use Agility instead of Strength.

weaponEnhancements.name: Spiritual Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is infused with spirit energy so it strikes at the soul instead of the body.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Attacks made with the weapon target Spiritual Defense instead of Physical Defense.

weaponEnhancements.name: Weighted
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is deliberately built heavier to hit harder.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Attacks deal +2 damage on a success, but the weapon gains the Heavy tag.

weaponEnhancements.name: Lightweight
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is made from a lighter material without losing its edge or durability.
weaponEnhancements.notes: The weapon loses the "Heavy" tag.

weaponEnhancements.name: Defensive
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is specially built to block and deflect incoming blows.
weaponEnhancements.notes: The weapon gains the "Defensive" tag.

weaponEnhancements.name: Armor Piercing
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is designed to punch through defensive gear and protective equipment.
weaponEnhancements.notes: This weapon ignores Equipment Bonuses to defenses.

weaponEnhancements.name: Shapeshifting Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can switch between multiple forms to suit the situation.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Has 3 forms: 1 inconspicuous non-weapon form and 2 distinct weapon forms.

weaponEnhancements.name: Swiss-Army Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon includes an integrated tool or utility function beyond combat use.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Choose a utility skill; gain +1 Equipment bonus to checks using that skill while wielding the weapon.

weaponEnhancements.name: Bane Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is especially deadly against a chosen kind of enemy.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Deals +2 damage against enemies with a chosen trait, such as Spirit.

weaponEnhancements.name: High-Impact Ammunition
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon fires rounds that hit with enough force to shove targets backward.
weaponEnhancements.notes: On hit, enemies are also pushed back; distance increases at higher ranks.

weaponEnhancements.name: Instant Retrieval
weaponEnhancements.description: A thrown weapon can be recalled to the wielder's hand immediately after it is thrown.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Recall requires no action.

weaponEnhancements.name: Size Changing / Extending
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can grow, shrink, or elongate to fit the needs of the moment.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry; intended to increase size/reach, possibly with drawbacks in tight spaces.

weaponEnhancements.name: Sentient Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is alive or self-aware and can communicate with its wielder.
weaponEnhancements.notes: May speak aloud or communicate telepathically.

weaponEnhancements.name: Heart-Seeker
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is unusually precise at striking vital openings.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Attacks with this weapon score a critical hit if they exceed the TN by 2 or more.

weaponEnhancements.name: Energy Drain
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon siphons energy during combat and feeds it back to its wielder.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Absorbs energy on hit, or when used to defend against attacks.

weaponEnhancements.name: Freeze Beam
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can project a line of freezing cold in addition to its normal attacks.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Rank of Ice equals the weapon's skill rank minus 1; area increases at higher ranks.

weaponEnhancements.name: Lightning Launcher
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can project a line of lightning in addition to its normal attacks.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Handbook text appears to contain a copy/paste typo; likely intended to use Lightning rather than Ice.

weaponEnhancements.name: Flamethrowing Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can spew flame in a line or cone as an additional attack mode.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Rank of Flame equals the weapon's skill rank minus 1; area increases at higher ranks.

weaponEnhancements.name: Explosive Ammunition
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon fires ammunition that detonates on impact.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Deals damage to nearby creatures around the point of impact.

weaponEnhancements.name: Beast Bullets
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon fires spiritual creatures as ammunition to attack enemies.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Example given: Caster Shell #5 from Outlaw Star.

weaponEnhancements.name: Titan Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon is massively oversized and built for truly gigantic force.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry with minimal defined mechanics.

weaponEnhancements.name: Autonomous Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon can act and attack on its own without being directly wielded.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry with minimal defined mechanics.

weaponEnhancements.name: Gravity Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon manipulates gravity to empower swings and pull or repel enemies.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry; combines swing enhancement with attraction/repulsion effects.

weaponEnhancements.name: Seismic Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: A heavy weapon that creates shockwaves, tremors, and fissures in the ground.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry with minimal defined mechanics.

weaponEnhancements.name: Black Hole Shooter
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon fires miniature black holes as devastating projectiles.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry with minimal defined mechanics.

weaponEnhancements.name: Conceptual Weapon
weaponEnhancements.description: The weapon attacks a target on a deeper metaphysical level rather than only harming the body.
weaponEnhancements.notes: Draft-level entry; handbook notes ideas like attacking a target's "name," "identity," or "existence."
